name: Fresh Tomato Risotto
recipeIngredient: 2 cups fresh tomato sauce (or 2 pounds ripe tomatoes, 1 tablespoon olive oil, and 2-3 minced garlic cloves)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large non-stick sauté pan, saute the onion in olive oil and butter with a dash of salt until it begins to turn golden.
2. In separate skillets, heat the vegetable broth and tomato sauce (or prepared tomatoes) just below a simmer.
3. If using fresh tomatoes: Scald them and peel, then chop or process in a blender for a few seconds. Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan, saute minced garlic for 2 minutes, then add tomatoes and cook down for about 15 minutes.
4. Add the rice to the sauteed onion and stir gently in the pan for about 2 minutes.
5. Pour in the white wine and stir until absorbed.
6. Gradually add heated broth a ladle at a time, stirring constantly, until nearly all is absorbed into the rice.
7. Repeat with tomato sauce and herbs, continuing to alternate broth and sauce until used up, then finish with more broth until the rice is al dente (about 25 minutes total).
8. Stir in hot remaining broth and grated Parmesan cheese just before serving.
9. Serve immediately, garnished with slivered basil and toasted pine nuts. Pass additional Parmesan on the side.
